title: The Free-Trade Paradox of Modern Anonymity
subtitle: 自由贸易悖论：为何我们感激陌生人的商品却不感激他们

paragraphs.en: Trade is one of the policy areas where the hostility that exists between populists and classical liberals is most visible. Free-traders point to the undeniable good that tariff-free trade has done for consumers across the world and to the observable alleviation of poverty in corners of the world where previously closed markets have been opened up. Protectionists point to the domestic producers who've paid the price for this globalized economy in the form of lost livelihoods and hollowed-out communities.
paragraphs.zh: 贸易是民粹主义者与古典自由主义者之间敌对最为明显的政策领域之一。自由贸易者指出，免关税贸易为全球消费者带来了不可否认的好处，并且在那些曾封闭市场得以开放的角落，贫困现象得到了显著缓解。保护主义者则指向那些为全球化经济付出代价的国内生产者，他们失去了生计，社区也变得空心化。

paragraphs.en: The ongoing conservative civil war often degenerates into content-free tribal warfare, but trade is a rare exception. There are substantial, thought-out policy proposals on both sides of the argument. Consequently, trade as a topic of discussion provides an opportunity for liberals and populists to have a real meeting of minds.
paragraphs.zh: 持续不断的保守派内斗常沦为毫无实质内容的部落战争，但贸易是一个罕见的例外。争论双方都提出了实质性的、深思熟虑的政策建议。因此，贸易作为讨论话题，为自由主义者和民粹主义者提供了真正达成共识的机会。

paragraphs.en: Strange as it might sound, the problem with trade in the modern world isn't a matter of dollars and cents. It's a matter of false consciousness. This observation was first made by Alexis de Tocqueville in 1840. Tracking the economic development of mankind from primitive to modern societies, Tocqueville observed a paradox unfolding over the centuries.
paragraphs.zh: 听起来或许奇怪，现代世界的贸易问题并非关乎金钱多少，而是关乎一种“错误意识”。这一观察最早由亚历克西斯·德·托克维尔于1840年提出。托克维尔追踪了人类从原始社会到现代社会的经济发展，观察到几个世纪以来一个悖论逐渐显现。

paragraphs.en: In primitive societies, the division of labor was as yet undeveloped, requiring each person, family, or tribe to be relatively independent in meeting their own basic material needs. Men built their own dwellings, farmed their own land, tended to their own livestock. This is not to deny that basic forms of trade took place, but our primitive ancestors lived fairly self-reliant lives.
paragraphs.zh: 在原始社会，劳动分工尚不发达，每个人、每个家庭或部落需相对独立地满足自身的基本物质需求。人们自己建造住所，耕种自己的土地，照料自己的牲畜。这并非否认基本贸易形式的存在，但我们的原始祖先过着相当自力更生的生活。

paragraphs.en: However, the exclusively local and face-to-face nature of economic and political organization constantly impressed upon these primitive peoples the uncontrollable reality of others and their needs. As Tocqueville notes, a man begins to perceive that he is not so independent of his fellow men as he had first imagined. At the advent of the modern world, the division of labor spread further throughout society.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，古代世界中经济与政治组织纯粹地方化、面对面进行的特性，不断提醒这些原始人类一个不可控的现实：他人的存在及其需求。正如托克维尔所指出的，一个人开始意识到自己并不像最初想象的那样独立于同胞。随着现代世界的到来，劳动分工在社会中日益蔓延。

paragraphs.en: Each person became more and more dependent on others for their basic needs. And yet, robbed of the engagement with our neighbors and with our local communities, we feel ourselves to be more and more independent of one another. As we become more and more dependent on others, we become less and less conscious of our dependence on others.
paragraphs.zh: 每个人的基本需求都越来越依赖他人。然而，由于我们不再像祖先那样因环境所迫而与邻居和当地社区频繁互动，我们反而觉得自己越来越独立于他人。我们越是依赖他人，就越意识不到自己对他人的依赖。

words.meaning: adj. 意识到的；觉察到的

paragraphs.en: This is the paradox of trade in the modern world. The false consciousness that this paradox generates causes havoc on the debates we have about free trade. There is scarcely a single commodity in any American household that isn't dependent for its manufacture and sale on scores of different people spread out across the entire globe.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是现代世界贸易的悖论。这一悖论所催生的错误意识，严重扰乱了我们对自由贸易的讨论。任何美国家庭中，几乎没有一件商品在制造和销售环节上不依赖于遍布全球的无数不同的人。

paragraphs.en: But as Tocqueville already foresaw, we do not feel dependent on these strangers for our way of life. No sense of the dependence of our own material welfare on their work ever strikes our national consciousness. We rarely contemplate the globalized avenues of free trade with gratitude.
paragraphs.zh: 但正如托克维尔早已预见的，我们并未觉得自己生活方式依赖于这些陌生人。我们的民族意识中，从未闪现过自身物质福祉有赖于他们劳动的感觉。我们很少怀着感激之情去思考自由贸易的全球化通路。

paragraphs.en: There are two reasons for this. The first, to put it bluntly, is money. Money allows us to purchase the work of others without giving any thought to them as human beings. Unlike our ancestors, we rarely have to meet face-to-face the people who've invented, built, shipped, or supplied our goods.
paragraphs.zh: 这背后有两个原因。第一个原因，直白地说，就是金钱。金钱让我们能够购买他人的劳动成果，而无需将他们当作有血有肉的人来考虑。与我们的祖先不同，我们几乎无需与发明、建造、运输或供应我们商品的人面对面打交道。

paragraphs.en: No relationship has to be built before an exchange can take place. Simply agree on a price, and you can have any goods you wish without taking a second thought for the human being involved. In this way, money makes us feel more independent than we actually are.
paragraphs.zh: 交易无需建立关系即可进行。只需就价格达成一致，你便能得到任何想要的商品，而无需对交易另一端的人稍加思索。就这样，金钱让我们感觉自己比实际更加独立。

paragraphs.en: Money gives us a set of rituals to perform and promises that if we do so we'll be able to wield power over others. The illusion is created that having enough money to buy something is the equivalent of knowing how to make it yourself. Gratitude for the anonymous men and women who make up the supply chain rarely makes its way into our consciousness.
paragraphs.zh: 金钱赋予我们一套仪式，并承诺只要我们照做，就能对他人施加权力。一种错觉由此产生：有足够的钱买某样东西，就等同于自己知道如何制作它。我们对构成供应链的那些无名男女的感激之情，几乎从未进入我们的意识。

paragraphs.en: Anonymity, in fact, is the second root cause of the free-trade paradox. Modernity has emancipated everyone from the limits of location and community. By and large, when we trade, we trade with strangers; when we vote, we vote for strangers.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，匿名性是自由贸易悖论的第二个根本原因。现代性将每个人都从地域和社群的限制中解放了出来。总体而言，我们交易时是与陌生人交易；投票时是为陌生人投票。

paragraphs.en: As opposed to the ancestors Tocqueville compares us to, we do not know the people with whom we have to do, in either the economic or the political sphere. This is simply the shadow side of the miracle of markets, which have allowed strangers to look after each other. This is how Tocqueville describes us: Each, standing apart, is like a stranger to the destiny of others.
paragraphs.zh: 与托克维尔用来与我们对比的祖先不同，我们无论在政治领域还是经济领域，都不了解那些与我们打交道的人。这不过是市场奇迹的阴暗面——市场让陌生人得以相互关照。托克维尔这样描述我们：每个人都孤立地站着，对他人的命运犹如陌生人。

paragraphs.en: His children and personal friends forming for him the entire human race. As for the remainder of his fellow citizens, he is beside them, but he does not see them. He touches them, but he does not feel them. The last sentence is an apt summary of how each of us functions in the modern economy: "He touches them, but he does not feel them."
paragraphs.zh: 他的子女和私人朋友构成了他的整个人类。至于其余的同胞，他就在他们身旁，却视而不见。他触碰他们，却感觉不到他们。最后一句话恰如其分地总结了我们在现代经济中的运作方式：“他触碰他们，却感觉不到他们。”

paragraphs.en: This is the greatest challenge facing defenders of free trade. It's exceedingly difficult for human beings to feel gratitude toward strangers, and the global marketplace that has made us so rich has also made us strangers to one another. Our brains are hardwired for tribal life, and tribes do not take kindly to strangers.
paragraphs.zh: 这是自由贸易捍卫者面临的最大挑战。人类极难对陌生人产生感激之情，而让我们变得如此富有的全球市场，也让我们彼此变成了陌生人。我们的大脑天生适合部落生活，而部落对陌生人并不友善。

paragraphs.en: If free-traders are going to win policy arguments in the future, they'll have to find a way of forging bonds of affection between American consumers and foreign producers. Only by de-anonymizing the men and women who supply us and by creating a sense of solidarity across borders can free trade win the day.
paragraphs.zh: 如果自由贸易者要在未来赢得政策辩论，就必须找到在美国消费者和外国生产者之间建立情感纽带的途径。唯有消除那些为我们提供商品和服务的人的匿名性，并建立超越国界的团结感，自由贸易才能获胜。

paragraphs.en: Otherwise, the inborn biological upper hand that protectionists have in the form of nationalist solidarity is bound to win the day at the ballot box.
paragraphs.zh: 否则，保护主义者以民族主义团结形式所拥有的先天生物学优势，注定将在投票箱前赢得胜利。
